statement.en: TingIS: Real-time Risk Event Discovery from Noisy Customer Incidents at Enterprise Scale
statement.summary: Real-time detection and mitigation of technical anomalies are critical for large-scale cloud-native services, where even minutes of downtime can result in massive financial losses and diminished user trust. While customer incidents serve as a vital signal for discovering risks missed by monitoring, extracting actionable intelligence from this data remains challenging due to extreme noise, high throughput, and semantic complexity of diverse business lines. In this paper, we present TingIS, an end-to-end system designed for enterprise-grade incident discovery. At the core of TingIS is a multi-stage event linking engine that synergizes efficient indexing techniques with Large Language Models (LLMs) to make informed decisions on event merging, enabling the stable extraction of actionable incid

proposer: Jun Wang, Ziyin Zhang, Rui Wang et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: TingIS is a 2026-dated system paper describing an operational architecture for incident discovery. The work is empirically grounded (enterprise deployment context) but the abstract is incomplete and key validation claims are cut off. The system itself exists/functions (can-be-made status), but scientific claims about effectiveness, generalization, and theoretical contributions remain unvalidated in the provided fragment. This is characteristic of preprint/provisional research awaiting full peer review.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: ノイズと言語モデルの共鳴が、統合という触媒を通して真の信号へと昇華する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 雑音の粒子たちが紫色の経路に沿って収束し、中心の金の点——真実の信号——へと統合される。
